item.title: Hegseth says Iran leader wounded as US vows to intensify strikes
item.description: EU hits back at Trump administration’s decision to lift sanctions on Russian oil

item.title: How Iran’s fightback surprised the US
item.description: Tehran has done ‘more with less’ as it hit major targets around the Gulf, but its firing has begun to wane

item.title: How to handle small irritants at work before they become big problems
item.description: Many workplace problems begin not with open conflict, but with small, unspoken irritants that quietly accumulate, writes Merge Gupta-Sunderji

item.description: Europeans look for ways to restart energy shipments as Iran’s new supreme leader vows to keep strait shut

item.description: Carmaker’s technology means EVs can be ready almost as quickly as filling a fuel tank

item.description: Britain’s first Black female publisher on reshaping the literary landscape, why editing is like midwifery — and her roll-call of authors, from Roy Heath to James Ellroy

item.description: The country needs incentives and investment to encourage and grow innovation

item.description: Government will adopt proposals from Fingleton review, to the dismay of ecologists

item.title: Trump’s Inexcusable Unpreparedness for the Iranian Oil Crisis
item.description: In the President’s first term, Iran demonstrated what tactics it would use in a confrontation with the U.S. Yet the Administration seems to have no game plan.

item.description: Albertans, especially those on the centre-right, should back the idea

item.description: The country missed out a decade ago, but changing geopolitics and rising demand for natural gas present new export opportunities

item.description: If renminbi settlement becomes cheaper and more trade migrates, firms will have less need to generate or hold US currency
